sections.paragraph:
παραυτίκα adv.=παραυτά (Trag., Hdt. et al.; SIG 495, 62; 68; oft. pap; Tob. 4:14; Ps 69:4; Jos., Ant, 9, 147; 12, 138; SibOr 13, 143; Just., D. 120, 6. On the spelling s. B-D-F §12, 3; Rob. 297) pert. to a point of time immediately subsequent to another point of time, on the spot, immediately, for the present used w. the art. preceding, as an adj. (Thu. 8, 82, 1 τὴν παραυτίκα ἐλπίδα; X., Cyr. 2, 2, 24 αἱ π. ἡδοναί; Pla., Phdr. 239a τὸ π. ἡδύ; Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 127 §531 ἡ π. ὀργή=the anger of the moment; Philo, Praem. 103; POxy 1381, 191f τ. π. καιρόν) τὸ π. ἐλαφρὸν τῆς θλίψεως slight momentary trouble 2 Cor 4:17.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

oft. oft. = often

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

art. art. = article

adj. adj. = adjective

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 772
